text: Orson's Warning
circa 10 ABY
Jessalyn Valios
Myrkr
Jessalyn Valios, Simon Sezirok
Jedi, Selas, Smuggler (Karrde Group)
Ah, doomed love on Myrkr....
Cleared Area (Before Main House) - Karrde's Base - Myrkr

Central to the base is this open area between the main buildings is this%ropen expanse, with its meticulously trimmed bluish-green grass and the%roccasional dotting of wildflowers. When necessary this area can be used%rfor anything from special (and discreet) picnic-type gatherings to the%rorganization of certain cargo before and after shipping. Often the%rclearing is the location of Karrde Group employees exercising, playing%rsome simple lawn games, or simply enjoying the outdoors in between duty%rshifts. The main house is just to the southeast of the clearing; far to%rthe west is the hanger, while the barracks are situated against the%rtrees to the north.
The Players:

Simon:%rBefore you is a young human male of average height and narrow%rbuild. His hair is a deep brown, parted and cut short. A strong jawline%rand deepset eyes of blue-gray give the man a stern look at a glance. For%rfacial hair he wears a well groomed goatee and mustache, trimmed short%rand of the same deep color as the rest of his hair. All in all, the%rman's demeanor can be summed up in a word: aware.%rSimon is dressed in earth tones. Light tan, loose fitting trousers%rare tucked into soft leather boots that come up to just under his knees,%rand are tied tight with brown, leather chords. Tucked into the top of%rhis pants is a simple shirt of a matching color. Over this is a loose%rwool tunic of dark brown, covering his arms completely and hanging down%rbelow his waste. It's comfortable clothing, suitable for most climates%rand cultures.%rStrapped diagnolly across his chest and back is what appears to be%rsome sort of harness. It's worn in the way some people wear a bandolier,%ryet there is nothing attached to the device. A long shaft of cylinder%rrises over his left shoulder, a rod sheathed where some warriors sling%rtheir sword.

Jessalyn:%rThe composure of this young human woman is probably the most striking%rthing about her. Though otherwise unassuming, her expression is one of%rsurprising coherence and calm, belied only by the slightly mischievous%rgleam in her leaf green eyes. Shining dark red hair falls in unruly%rsilken waves down to the middle of her back, framing her wide cheekbones%rand smooth, pale skin not as fragile as most redheads'. She is%rrelatively tall for a human woman, with long-boned limbs and a natural%rgrace amplified by her skills.

Jessa is dressed in a drab green sleeveless shirt, and a pair of kakhi%rpants with plenty of pockets. Around her waist is a black leather%rutility belt. Her hair is held back from her face and clipped behind her%rhead, though stray curling locks continue to fall into her eyes. The fit%rof her trousers and the sturdy brown suede boots on her legs emphasize%rher narrow waist and the long-legged rhythm of her strides.

The crickets and insects fill the night with their chirping and%rcacophony while the stars twinkle brightly in a perfectly clear%rtwilight. Once more seeking the meditative properties of the outdoors,%rSimon walks around the compound, his arms clasped behind his back. He%rlooks up to the night sky, enjoying the feel of a cool breeze as much as%rthe silence and peace that numbs his normally enhanced senses. There was%ra melancholy beauty to the night, and Simon let the long shadows and%rdarkness of the eve wrap him in a cool embrace.

The darkness is all-enveloping, wrapping around the compound like a%rvelvety blanket, and masking Jessalyn's movements as she walks silently%racross the grass, her light-footed grace an asset in stealth. She%rwatches Simon with a melancholy expression of her own as he moves%rthrough the clearing, hugging the trees so that he won't notice her at%rfirst. But when he pauses to look up at the night sky, she catches her%rbreath as the moonlight catches his face, and her heart wrenches. She%rsteps away from the trees toward him, her figure emerging almost like a%rghost out of the blackness. "Simon," she speaks his name softly to get%rhis attention.

Closing his eyes, Simon basks in the starlight a handful of heartbeats%rbefore turning to the sound of Jessalyn's voice. A small, secretive%rsmile spreads on his lips. His eyes are sharp as they move toward the%rJedi, shining as if they still held some of the star light he'd been%renjoying.
"Jessa," Simon responds, his voice pitched low as if to keep from%rshattering the stillness of the night. "I didn't know you were near."

She smiles slightly, looking younger in the soft lighting, her eyes the%ronly thing old in her face. They're heavy with worry that she seems%reager to hide, judging by the persistent and genuine smile as she gazes%rup at Simon. "I didn't mean to startle you," she says in equally quiet%rtones. "But I'm glad you're here." Jessalyn has wanted to talk to him,%reven though it's a conversation that she dreads, and even now part of%rher wants to turn on her heel and seek refuge back in the barracks. Her%rheart, however, will not allow that. She takes a few courageous steps%rtoward Simon.

Even without the True Source, even in the dark of night, Simon could%rread apprehension about Jessalyn as she walks closer to him. He couldn't%rquite put his finger on what it was that made him see that, exactly. She%rwas as beautiful as he'd ever seen her. He could lose himself in her%rforest colored eyes. Extending a hand to her, he says, cocking his head%rto one side, "You did not startle me, Jessa. What's wrong? You seem...%rnervous?"

Jessalyn slips her cool hand into Simon's and turns her face up to the%rstars as well, letting her vision fill with the calming image of the sky%ras dark whisps of clouds are blown back and forth to hide and then%rreveal the milky clusters of stars. "We're going to be leaving soon,"%rshe says finally, with a heavy sigh. "I find myself wondering about what%rwill happen then. I mean. About you... and me." She bites down on her%rlower lip, thinking that had sounded calmer and more reasonable than it%rdid in her head. Risking a glance, she peers up at him from beneath the%rdark screen of her lashes.

Simon bites his lower lip and lowers his head, his gaze looking at the%rdelicate hand clasped in his own. He intertwines his fingers with hers,%rmaking the union of their hands more complete. With the shadows of the%rnight, it is difficult to tell which fingers are Simon's and which are%rJessalyn's. A fitting image for what Simon was about to propose.

"I want to be one with you," he says at last, turning his eyes back to%rher face. His throat was suddenly dry, and his voice cracks slightly as%rhe continues, "You spoke of an intimacy once, which I feared. I want you%rto teach me this when we leave here, Jessa. I want to share my soul with%ryou."

Her fingers automatically tighten around his when he speaks, and Jessa%rturns her face towards him, eyes rounded now with shock. The words that%rshe had wanted to speak are lost on her tongue, and she swallows hard%rbefore finding her voice, shifting her stance so that she's facing him%rmore directly. "Are you sure? Simon, when we leave here ... I'm afraid%rthat I'll just be a Jedi to you again, and not a woman." It was ironic%rthat as much as she yearned to leave this place, to be able to feel the%rForce and have it strengthen her once more, she was afraid of doing so%rbecause of Simon. But she's determined to be honest with him. If they%rcan't meet halfway, then there's nothing here for her.
Her other hand lifts to cover their entwined fingers as she searches his%rface, her eyes revealing just how much she needs the fulfillment he%rspeaks of, a thrill going through her at the thought.

"Jedi... Selas... is that all that we are?" Simon asks, shaking his%rhead. His expression is sardonic for a moment, then shifts to one more%rserious. He says, "I love you, Jessalyn Valios." He pauses to give the%rstatement more weight, then continues, "I know what my vision means now,%rI think. You and I are meant to unite and be more. There are things we%rcan teach each other. I do not have all the answers, and neither do the%rJedi. Join me... be one with me... and we will make a new order. We will use%rthe True Source... the Force... to heal the galaxy."

This was nothing like the Dark Side, Jessalyn thinks to herself. The%rDark Side was fear and hatred, not love and cooperation, and yet... the%rimplications of his words suggest to her possibilities of knowledge and%rpower no other Jedi has ever known, and the prospect is oddly%rintoxicating to a woman who has never been motivated by a thirst for%rpower. Knowledge, however, and the melding of her being with a vibrant%rand noble man, those were things that found a strong answer inside her.

A smile trembles on Jessalyn's lips as she nods her head, too emotional%rto speak at first, moving closer so that she can feel the warmth from%rhis body. "And to heal each other," she adds in a soft voice. "There was%ra reason, I think, that it was you who touched my mind while I was still%rsleeping, and you who finally freed me. I love you, Simon."

Releasing Jessalyn's hand, he folds his arms around the woman and draws%rher closer to him. He lowers his face to her hair, inhaling the sweet%rfragrance of her. Closing his eyes, he commits every sensation and%rfeeling to memory, hoping to store away the moment forever. In that%rmoment, he was one with the woman he loved, like two sparks that had%rcome together and become a flame to fight back the darkness. An image%rfrom his memory fills his mind suddenly, of a flame beating beating back%rthe darkness even as it tries to consume the source of the light. He can%rnot remember where he'd seen the image before, but for a moment, his%rfeelings are tinged with sadness.
Drawing away from Jessalyn enough so that he can look clearly into her%rface, Simon asks, "When is it that we will leave this beautiful place,%rJessa? Will it be soon, then?"

Jessa tilts her head thoghtfully as she returns his gaze, keeping her%rarms easily around him. "Soon, yes," she agrees, suddenly as eager as he%ris. "When our ship is ready and we know where we want to go first for%rcertain. I'm ready to leave, even though it is beautiful here...." She%rtrails off, thinking that isn't quite true. There's something about%rbeing able to focus only on her physical senses here that is alluring,%rreminding her of the simplicity and pleasures of her youth, before life%rhad been complicated by knowledge of the Force.
And here, when she looked into Simon's eyes, there were no shadows.
Jessa moves her hands up to Simon's shoulders and pulls him down so that%rshe can kiss him very softly. "I didn't expect this, you know," she adds%rwith a breathless smile.

Simon opens his eyes slowly after Jessa's lips leave his. It was an odd%rthing, when Simon stopped to think about it. He didn't know why he%ralways closed his eyes when Jessa kissed him. Perhaps the sweetness, the%rtenderness, overwhelmed all his senses such that he couldn't bear to see%ranything else. Perhaps it was that he wanted to commit each brief touch%rto memory, perfect in and of itself.

Swallowing, Simon returns the look Jessalyn is giving him, then says, "I%rdidn't expect any of this. I didn't expect to fall in love with you in a%rplace so much like my home. Some surprises in life are pleasant."

A chuckle shakes her body as Jessalyn leans into him and slides her arms%raround his neck, bending back her head back so that she can smile up%rat him from that perspective. "You're bound to find plenty more of%rsurprises," she says ruefully. "There's a lot for us to accomplish in a%rshort amount of time. But I want to fight beside you. I want to share%reverything with you." She speaks the last sentence in a softer voice,%rlashes lowering almost shyly. "I don't want you to ever regret... what I%rfeel for you."

Joy fills Simon. A surge of energy starts from his belly and moves out%rfrom his center, tightening his muscles like a coiled spring, until he%rcan hold it back no more. Scooping up Jessalyn in his arms, he lifts her%rup into the air. A boystrous laugh bubbles out of him, and he turns%rabout, enjoying the site of Jessalyn's face above him, framed by the%rstarry night sky.
As he lowers her back to the ground, he brings his hands up to caress%rher cheeks and touch her hair lightly. He says, his voice suddenly%rquiet, "I could never regret what has happened between us."

Laughing, Jessalyn wiggles her feet back and forth as she's swept up%rinto the air, adrenaline suddenly rushing through her as her heart%rraces. She doesn't cling to him as if she might be afraid he will let go%ror drop her; her arms are embracing and trusting as she grins back down%rinto his face, the stars casting silvery glints off her hair, forming a%rsoft halo around her features. When her feet are back on the ground, she%rremains close to him, suppressing effervescent giggles that vibrate from%rher throat as he caresses her hair. "You're crazy," she says%raffectionately. "It's a good thing I like crazy." She eyes him coyly,%rthe tensing of her body perceptible even in the darkness.

In many ways, the one known as Simon Sezirok truly was insane, though%rlikely not in the fashion that Jessalyn was playfully teasing. He had a%rzealot's narrow minded obsession. He was focused and straight forward,%rlike an arrow fired straight and true toward its target. At this point%rin time, that target was Jessalyn. Nothing could draw him away from her%rat this point, even for a moment.

Taking Jessalyn's tensing for hesitation, Simon lowers his face to%rJessalyn's once more, pressing his lips to hers in a far more passionate%rkiss than what they'd shared before. The jubilation and energy he felt%rthat caused him to lift Jessa into the air was still within him, still%rsending his heart to racing. A need was building within him, primal and%rnatural. Drawing from the kiss, Simon says, his voice husky, "I want%ryou, sweet Jessa. If we can not share our souls here, then let us share%rwhat we can. This place. This night."

Maybe the circumstances that had led up until now had made it impossible%rfor Jessalyn to avoid becoming the target of Simon's obsession; perhaps%rtheir personalities and ideals were so strong that they were inexorably%rlinked together, like the vision he had shared with her, the thorny vine%rheavy with roses twined around the sturdy wooden staff. Whatever the%rreasons, she has given up resisting, her soul yearning for what he and%rno one else has ever offered her. She catches her breath as she recovers%rfrom the fiery kiss, her cheeks flushed and hot, but her smile%rbrightening as she nudges her nose against his. She can barely hear her%rown whisper above the pounding of the blood in her ears. "I want you,%rtoo," she murmurs, drawing him with her as she glances towards the%rcovering of the trees, sneaking a kiss every few steps until the forest%rswallows both of them up in darkness.
Simon and Jessalyn
